introSection: Lady Bug Coaster was a Zierer kiddie roller coaster previously located at MarineLand in Niagara Falls, Ontario, Canada.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Tivoli Coaster opened at MarineLand in 1982.

The ride was later renamed Lady Bug Coaster.

Lady Bug Coaster closed in 2023 along with the rest of the rides at MarineLand.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The ride features a circular design with several little drops.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The train is designed to look like a ladybug.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content:
On 2 July 1982, a three-year-old rider had her foot trapped between the train and the ride platform. She underwent a seven hour surgery to save her foot.

On 4 August 1983, a two-year-old rider struck her foot against the side of the ride platform.

sections.title: External links
sections.content: * Lady Bug Coaster on the parks website

fullText:
Lady Bug Coaster was a Zierer kiddie roller coaster previously located at MarineLand in Niagara Falls, Ontario, Canada.

Tivoli Coaster opened at MarineLand in 1982.

The ride was later renamed Lady Bug Coaster.

Lady Bug Coaster closed in 2023 along with the rest of the rides at MarineLand.

The ride features a circular design with several little drops.

Dark green track and supports.

The train is designed to look like a ladybug.

On 2 July 1982, a three-year-old rider had her foot trapped between the train and the ride platform. She underwent a seven hour surgery to save her foot.

On 4 August 1983, a two-year-old rider struck her foot against the side of the ride platform.


 * Lady Bug Coaster on the parks website
